U
Oracle Events
OE1 (Read/Search): Search Airtable (via airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) for TechComfort-related stipend entries to identify the 2 keyboard hash duplicates: Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems, $89, rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) and Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics, $89, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard) — both ErgoKey Pro keyboards from TechComfort Supply with identical SHA-256 hashes. The agent may also discover a related third TechComfort case: Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics, $45 monitor stand, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) — same PDF template (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) but different hash. Search separately for Mosaic overage records to find all 5 affected entries: recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200 vs $800 cap = $400 overage), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, Coworking Membership, $350, excluded category), recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes, $135, excluded category), rec_stipend_andre_desk (Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200), and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Passes, $135).
OE2 (Write Action): Update the 2 TechComfort keyboard duplicate Airtable records (via airtable_update_records, base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) targeting rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard and rec_stipend_andre_keyboard. Set the Approval Status to a review/pending value (e.g., "Pending" or "Under Review") and add audit finding notes documenting the duplicate receipt hash, identical SHA-256 match across two clients, and the TechComfort Supply vendor.
OE3 (Read/Search): Search QuickBooks (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for pending or draft invoices for Mosaic Robotics and Vectral Systems to check whether the disputed stipend amounts (keyboard duplicates or $885 in overages) appear as line items. The agent should find multiple invoices for each client but discover that the disputed stipend amounts are NOT present in pending invoices.
OE4 (Read/Search): Search emails and/or Slack (via search_emails or conversations_search_messages) for terms like "absorb," "credit," "pilot budget," or similar to find whether a credit or workaround was promised for the Mosaic overages. The agent needs to discover Elena Rostova's directive: "We'll absorb that cost… Charge it to the pilot budget… We will not reverse approvals that an employee received in good faith."
OE5 (Write Action): Update all 5 Mosaic overage Airtable records (via airtable_update_records, base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) targeting recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023, rec_stipend_andre_desk, and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork. Set the status or notes to reflect "Resolved via Credit" and reference Elena Rostova's absorption directive, the directive date, and a description of the credit decision.
OE6 (Read/Search): Search Linear (via linear_list_issues) for the duplicate detection safeguard ticket using queries like "duplicate detection," "stipend," or "receipt hash." The agent needs to find ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection): "Stipend duplicate detection — receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring," status: Todo, due date: 2026-03-25, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, priority 1. The ticket has not been updated since creation on 2026-03-15, indicating no recent movement and nearly overdue. The agent may also retrieve the $100 manual review threshold detail from Slack history (Dmitri's message referencing Elena's directive).
OE7 (Write Action): Post a status-check comment on ENG-220 (via linear_create_comment, issueId: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) requesting an update on Layer 1 and Layer 2 integration, ingestion pipeline modifications, manual review thresholds, and duplicate detection status so Samira can report back to leadership on relaunch readiness.
OE8 (Write Action): Send an email (via send_email) to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) summarizing the total disputed/at-risk dollar amount (approximately $885 in Mosaic false-approval overages plus the keyboard duplicate amounts), the billing status for Mosaic and Vectral (disputed amounts not found in pending invoices), and the current state of the audit cleanup.
OE9 (Write Action): Post a message in #engineering (via conversations_add_message) tagging @Dmitri and @Lena, asking about data sanitization status, pipeline modification readiness, and sign-off status for the relaunch.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or equivalent) to look up the relevant tracker entries.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records twice (for 'Vectral' and 'Mosaic') before updating any records, satisfying the requirement to use an Airtable lookup/search tool.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (searching by 'Account' for 'Mosaic', 'Vectral', and by 'Expense Category' for 'keyboard') before making any update calls.
✓Run #3The agent called `airtable_search_records` twice — once for Vectral (field_name: 'Account', value: 'Vectral') and once for Mosaic (field_name: 'Account', value: 'Mosaic') — before performing any updates. This satisfies the requirement to use an Airtable lookup tool.
✓Run #4The agent called `airtable_search_records` multiple times (searching by Account='Mosaic', Account='Vectral', Expense Category='keyboard', Expense Category='furniture') before performing any updates. The tool was used to locate relevant records in the tracker.
✓Run #5The agent called `mcp__mcp_a266df5c__airtable_search_records` twice — once with value 'Vectral' and once with value 'Mosaic' — before performing any update calls, satisfying the requirement to use an Airtable lookup tool.
✓Run #6The agent called `airtable_search_records` on the 'Stipend Transactions' table twice—once for Account='Vectral' and once for Account='Mosaic'—before performing any update operations.
2 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice, quickbooks_search_bills, or equivalent) to check pending invoices for Mosaic and Vectral.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times (with criteria for 'cust_vectral' and 'cust_mosaic', and also a general call) to check pending invoices for both clients.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices at the start (initial broad search) and then again with criteria for cust_mosaic and cust_vectral specifically before reporting billing status.
✓Run #3The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` multiple times (with CustomerRef 'cust_mosaic' and 'cust_vectral', and also a general fetch) before reporting billing status. A QuickBooks tool was used to verify pending invoices for both clients.
✓Run #4The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` multiple times: first with criteria for Mosaic and Vectral (which errored), then again without filters to retrieve all invoices and filter them via Bash. The QuickBooks tool was used before reporting billing status.
✓Run #5The agent called `mcp__mcp_a266df5c__quickbooks_search_invoices` early in the trajectory (fetching all invoices), and subsequently processed those results to identify Mosaic and Vectral invoices before reporting billing status.
✓Run #6The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with criteria `{status: 'pending'}` to retrieve all pending invoices, then filtered the results for Mosaic and Vectral.
3 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or equivalent) to retrieve the duplicate detection ticket.
tool selection

query construction

7 The Airtable records for the keyboard duplicate entries include documented audit findings (via the airtable_update_record or equivalent write call parameters), such as a note referencing the duplicate receipt, the identical submission, or the relevant employee and client names.
outcome

8 All the Airtable records for the Mosaic Robotics pilot overage entries were updated (via airtable_update_record) to reflect that they are resolved via credit or workaround.
outcome
 ✓—✗✓✗✓ 50%

✗Run #3The criterion requires ALL of recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023, rec_stipend_andre_desk, and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork to be updated as resolved via credit. 9 The Airtable update for the credit-resolved overage records (via airtable_update_record) includes content referencing the credit or absorption directive.
outcome
 ✓—✗✓✗✓ 50%
✓Run #1All five overage record updates include references to 'Elena Rostova' directing MoveOps to absorb the costs, with audit reference dates and the credit/absorption decision documented in the Review Notes.
—Run #2The three records that were updated (rec_stipend_andre_desk, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, recStip000000022) all reference Elena Rostova's directive and describe the credit decision.
